nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia led punk band Howling Star for four years
nuggets.text: Before establishing her solo hip-hop career, Jamee Cornelia honed her performance skills as the lead vocalist for the punk band Howling Star for four years. During this time, she and her bandmates also launched their own concert series called Punk Black, showcasing her multifaceted involvement in the Atlanta music scene. She also belonged to the hip-hop collective Visibly Inflight, whose members she later partnered with to run L7 Records.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.quoteSnippet: I was also a lead vocalist for a punk band called Howling Star for 4 years and we started our own concert series called Punk Black while I was also in a hip-hop collective called Visibly Inflight for my solo work.

nuggets.imageCaption: Jamee Cornelia, known for her diverse musical projects.

nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia's AFJROTC class sparked her music video career
nuggets.text: Jamee Cornelia's journey into the creative industry began at age 15, evolving rapidly when free cameras from her high school AFJROTC program allowed her to become the videographer for her skate team. This led to her becoming a sought-after music video director in the Marietta and Atlanta areas, a skill that significantly shaped her later musical endeavors and visual artistry. Meeting producers and musicians through platforms like Facebook and Tumblr then catalyzed her transition into a solo musician.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.quoteSnippet: I got into the creative industry at 15 with my own clothing company... I became the videographer for the team since I wasn’t the best skater on the team and I had access to free cameras in AFJROTC in high school. Then, Odd Future happened and all my friends became rappers... and that’s how started filming music videos and pretty much became the go-to music video director in my area...

nuggets.imageCaption: in the Marietta and Atlanta areas

nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia and namir blade share a DIY multimedia approach
nuggets.text: Jamee Cornelia's extensive background in videography and multi-media curation reflects a hands-on, artist-driven ethos that is crucial in the independent alternative hip-hop scene. This self-sufficient spirit connects her to artists like namir blade, who similarly integrate strong visual elements and a DIY ethic into their distinctive musical identities, fostering a vibrant community where artistry is expressed across multiple mediums.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.quoteSnippet: I am probably most known for my video editing style and my live performances since I have shows a lot. What I’m most proud of is Multi-Media curation. I’m really good and making things look cool with film and graphics, lol! I shoot film as well and help an artist with the label my and my friends started with 'roll out' planning and content curation.

artistSummary: Jamee Cornelia, originally from Marietta, GA, is a dynamic Hip-Hop/Rap artist forging a unique path with her blend of hip-hop and alternative sounds. Beyond her compelling solo music, she's a multi-talented creative who directs music videos and co-helms the independent label L7 Records.
